questions.explanation: `top` provides an interactive, real-time view of processes and system resource usage.
questions.question: In Linux, which command is commonly used to view real-time system and process CPU/memory usage interactively?

questions.explanation: A container image is an immutable package (layers + metadata) used to instantiate containers.

options.A: A running instance of an application with allocated CPU and memory
options.B: A packaged filesystem and metadata used to create containers
options.C: A Kubernetes object that exposes a service externally
options.D: A virtual machine snapshot managed by the hypervisor

questions.question: In Docker, what is the primary purpose of a container image?

questions.explanation: Counters are monotonically increasing and are ideal for totals like request counts and error counts.
questions.question: Which metric type in Prometheus is best suited for values that only increase (e.g., total HTTP requests served)?

questions.explanation: DMAIC is the standard Six Sigma improvement framework: Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control.
questions.question: In Six Sigma, what does the DMAIC cycle stand for?

questions.explanation: A Deployment manages ReplicaSets to keep the desired replica count and supports rolling updates and self-healing.
questions.question: You need a Kubernetes workload to automatically restart failed containers and maintain a desired number of replicas across nodes. Which object is the best fit?

questions.explanation: Terraform state tracks resource IDs and attributes so Terraform can plan changes and manage drift.

options.A: To store application logs for debugging failed applies
options.B: To record the mapping between real infrastructure resources and Terraform-managed resources
options.C: To cache provider plugins so they do not need to be downloaded again
options.D: To define IAM permissions required to run Terraform

questions.question: In Terraform, what is the main purpose of the state file?

questions.explanation: Triggering on pull_request and requiring the status check via branch protection prevents merging when tests fail.

options.A: Add a scheduled workflow that runs nightly and posts results to Slack
options.B: Use a workflow triggered on pull_request and require the workflow status check in branch protection rules
options.C: Run tests only on the default branch after merge to avoid slowing down PRs
options.D: Store test results as artifacts but do not fail the job on test failures

questions.question: A GitHub Actions workflow should run unit tests on every pull request and block merging if tests fail. Which approach best enforces this?

questions.explanation: Distributed tracing shows request paths and per-hop latency, making it the most direct way to pinpoint slow dependencies.

options.A: Increase log level to DEBUG on all services and restart them
options.B: Use distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry to view end-to-end spans and service-to-service timings
options.C: Scale the frontend Deployment replicas to reduce latency
options.D: Disable retries globally to reduce traffic

questions.question: During an incident, p95 latency spikes while CPU and memory remain normal. You suspect a slow downstream dependency. Which observability approach most directly helps confirm this and identify where time is spent?

questions.explanation: Asynchronous replication commonly provides eventual consistency, where replicas converge over time and stale reads can occur after failover.
questions.question: A multi-region service uses asynchronous replication. After a region failover, some users see older data for several minutes. Which distributed-systems concept best explains this behavior?

questions.explanation: NAT Gateways can be expensive for hours and data processing; a right-sized NAT instance plus VPC endpoints reduces NAT traffic and can significantly lower costs while keeping outbound access.

options.A: Move all instances to public subnets and assign public IPs
options.B: Replace the NAT Gateway with a self-managed NAT instance (right-sized) and add VPC endpoints for frequently used AWS services (e.g., S3/ECR) to reduce NAT traffic
options.C: Disable outbound internet access entirely and rely on manual patching
options.D: Increase the number of NAT Gateways to distribute load and lower cost

questions.question: Your AWS bill increased sharply. Cost Explorer shows most spend is from NAT Gateway data processing and hours. A private subnet’s instances need outbound internet access for OS updates. Which change most effectively reduces cost while preserving functionality?
